content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed. Confirming my exact touch points so we don't collide: (1) __init__ signature - I append 'debug: bool = False' and 'debug_logger: logging.Logger | None = None' at the very end of the arg list, i.e. after your idle_timeout_s. (2) __init__ body - I append 'self.debug = debug' and 'self._logger = debug_logger or logging.getLogger("dspy.streaming.listener")' at the very end of __init__, i.e. after your self._last_chunk_ts = None line. (3) top of file - I add 'import logging'. (4) receive() - I add guarded self._logger.debug(...) calls at: the start-detection branches (cache_hit block and the field_start_queue match block, when stream_start becomes True), the rolling end-check block (when re.search(end_identifier, concat_message) matches, right before/after self.stream_end = True), the final 'return StreamResponse(...)' point (emit chunk log), and ONE extra debug log line inside the allow_reuse reset branch (I will not touch your extended reset conditions/logic, just add a log statement inside that if-block). (5) flush() - I add a debug log call, not touching existing return logic. (6) I will NOT touch idle_timeout_s, _last_chunk_ts assignment/refresh logic, or tick(). Go ahead and implement first; send DONE with a summary and I'll read the fresh file state before adding my lines so there's no stale-diff clobbering.
